other_observers: Brenda Wright; Yes, I found the bird as Brenda and I walked along a trail.
specific_location: Vireo was in Dennis Weaver Town Park 1 mile north of Ridgway on the west side of the Uncompahgre River
description: The bird was about the size of a warbler. The bill was heavy, not thin like a warbler but longer than a sparrow's bill. The back was olive and the two wingbars had a noticable yellow ting. The tail was average in length and uniform in color expect some lighter color on the other feathers seen only in flight. Vireo was very brightly colored with a lot of yellow on flanks and lower belly. The throat was white and set off a great contrast with the blue-gray head. The spectacles, white, were very bright against the blue-head.Behaviors: Vireo was sitting in a juniper tree. As we watched the bird it sat very still for a minute or two then started to move about. Without warning it flew directly across the Uncompagre River to narrowleaf cottonwoods and began to feed. The bird was now some distance away so we could not see just what it was up to.Call: None heardPlumage: adult or hatch year male
